name: MongoDB Hackathon Standard Rubric
description: Default judging rubric for MongoDB DevRel hackathons. Four weighted criteria totaling 100% with a 10-point scale per criterion.

criteria.name: Innovation
criteria.description: How creative and novel is the solution? Does it solve a real problem in a unique way?

scoringGuide.3-4: Minor twist on a common idea
scoringGuide.5-6: Interesting approach with some novel elements
scoringGuide.7-8: Creative solution to a real problem, clearly differentiated
scoringGuide.9-10: Truly novel approach that could change how people think about the problem

criteria.name: Technical Complexity
criteria.description: How technically challenging is the implementation? Quality of code, architecture, and engineering decisions.

scoringGuide.3-4: Standard web app patterns, some integration work
scoringGuide.7-8: Complex system with thoughtful design, error handling, and performance considerations
scoringGuide.9-10: Exceptional engineering: distributed systems, real-time processing, or advanced algorithms

criteria.name: MongoDB Usage
criteria.description: How effectively does the project leverage MongoDB features? Includes Atlas Search, Vector Search, Aggregation Pipeline, Change Streams, Time Series, etc.

scoringGuide.1-2: Basic find/insert only, MongoDB as a simple key-value store
scoringGuide.3-4: Uses indexes and basic queries appropriately
scoringGuide.5-6: Leverages aggregation pipeline or Atlas Search
scoringGuide.7-8: Multiple MongoDB features used effectively (Vector Search + Aggregation, Change Streams, etc.)
scoringGuide.9-10: Deep, creative use of MongoDB capabilities that showcases the platform's strengths

criteria.name: Presentation
criteria.description: Clarity of demo, quality of documentation, UI/UX polish, and overall communication of the project's value.

scoringGuide.1-2: No demo or documentation, unclear what the project does
scoringGuide.5-6: Clear demo showing core features, adequate documentation
scoringGuide.7-8: Polished presentation, good storytelling, clean UI
scoringGuide.9-10: Exceptional presentation: compelling narrative, professional polish, clear call-to-action

settings.commentPrompt: Please provide specific feedback for the team (2-3 sentences minimum).
